notes.note: patres conscripti, the formal Senate address. The First Catilinarian was delivered in the temple of Jupiter Stator at the foot of the Palatine on 8 November 63 BC, with Catiline himself present in the chamber.
Quo usque tandem abutere, Catilina, patientia nostra? --- among the most-quoted opening lines in Latin oratory.

Temple of Jupiter Stator ("the Stayer"), founded by Romulus on the spot where Jupiter caused the Romans to halt and rally against the Sabines; chosen for this Senate meeting for its associations with rallying against an internal threat.
Cicero's first prosopopoeia in this speech: the personified patria addresses Catiline directly. A second prosopopoeia of the patria addresses Cicero himself near the end of the speech.
